architecture.summary: Deliberative Alignment teaches language models explicit safety specifications and trains them to recall and reason over those specifications before generating answers. The model is given safety policies as context and required to explicitly reason through adherence to those policies during inference, creating an interpretable decision pathway where the model's reasoning steps are observable before the final answer.
trained_components: answer generation conditioned on policy reasoning

biblio.title: Deliberative Alignment: Reasoning Enables Safer Language Models

claims.statement: Deliberative Alignment achieves highly precise adherence to OpenAI's safety policies without requiring human-written chain-of-thoughts or answers
Deliberative Alignment pushes the Pareto frontier by simultaneously increasing robustness to jailbreaks while decreasing overrefusal rates
Reasoning over explicitly specified policies improves out-of-distribution generalization
Reasoning over explicitly specified policies enables more scalable, trustworthy, and interpretable alignment
